originalTitle: Tunisia 1-3 Netherlands: World Cup 2026 – as it happened
summary: Minute-by-minute report: Brian Brobbey on target again as Dutch advance to last 32 and send Tunisia home pointless. Bryan Armen Graham was watching
content: That’s all for tonight. Thanks for following along with us and be sure to check out Ed Aarons’ match report from Kansas City. It’s official. The Netherlands will face Morocco in the last 32 after winning Group F (Guadalupe, Monday 29 June). Japan will face Brazil (Houston, Monday 29 June). Sweden will face France or Norway or Switzerland or Germany. There’s the whistle and the Dutch have done their part with a 3-1 win over Tunisia. Will they get the result they need in Arlington? It seems they’re only a few minutes away … 90+3 min Tunisia win a corner, but it’s cleared from danger easily. 90 min Tunisia make their final substitution, sending on goal-scorer Sebastian Tounekti to replace Hazem Mastouri. There will be five minutes of stoppage time. 86 min If these results hold for another few minutes, the Netherlands will top Group F with seven points while Japan and Sweden will both go through. 84 min Noa Lang comes on for Cody Gakpo. That’s the Netherlands’ fifth and final substitution. 80 min The Dutch win a free kick from about 25 yards. The effort ping-pongs around in the area before Aké’s final effort goes out wide of the post for a goal kick. 77 min Mejbri has a go from distance and Verbruggen fully extends to tip it over the crossbar. Now the Netherlands will make a fourth switch with Memphis Depay coming on for Brian Brobbey. 75 min Play has barely resumed by the time Tunisia make their fourth sub: Firas Chaouat coming on for Ismael Gharbi, who very nearly opened the scoring in the second minute. 72 min A couple of subs for the Netherlands on the other side of the water break. Summerville, Kluivert and Koopmeiners are in. Malen, Reijnders and De Jong are out. 69 min There’s a pause in play for another hydration break. 68 min Moments after Reijinders’ latest effort caroms off the crossbar, it’s a trio of substitutions for Tunisia. Ben Ouanes, Achouri and Mahmoud are coming on for Ben Hmida, Ben Slimane and Khedira. 64 min And Sweden have tied Japan on an Anthony Elanga goal. A lovely header by Jan-Paul van Hecke off Reijnders’ left-wing corner to the near post. This one might be changed to an own goal as it appeared to graze the head of Ben Slimane on its way into the top-right corner. 61 min The Netherlands win a corner and the rain is really coming down now. 57 min An just as play restarts, the news comes through from Arlington: Japan have gone 1-0 up against Sweden. There’s a loud reaction around the stadium as the score flashes up on the big screens. A gorgeous header into the bottom-left corner by Hazem Mastouri off a corner from Hannibal Mejbri, who earned that set piece himself by carrying the ball a long way rom the halfway line. Very poor marking by Reijnders, who lost his assignment. 51 min The rain is coming down a bit harder now. Dumfries chance from the center of the area is blocked by a Tunisia defense and subsequently cleared. 49 min Gakpo finds space on the edge of the area and clips a cross to the back post. Brobbey does well to keep it alive, but his return ball across goal is hacked clear by the Tunisia defense. 46 min And we’re off in the second half. A couple of emails from readers while we wait for kick-off. James, a Kansas City native, writes in with a lovely bit of local context: What makes the accounts of the Dutch takeover of KC so remarkable is how much the Hunt family, owners of the NFL’s Chiefs, and Lamar Hunt in particular, were instrumental in promoting the 1994 World Cup and eventually bringing professional soccer to KC. It is awesome to see that image of the Dutch filling the KC streets I think of as home. And Kári Tulinius is already looking ahead to the knockout-stage possibilities: If Norway beat France, which isn’t unthinkable, Sweden could meet Norway. That would be quite the grudge match. If Sweden won that Potter would probably be placed into the Swedish royal line of succession. Another own goal, another unwanted piece of World Cup history. Tunisia’s Ellyes Skhiri turned a low cross into his own net after just four minutes against the Netherlands, taking the tally of own goals at this year’s World Cup to 12. That equals the record for a men’s World Cup tournament, matching the total from 2018 – and this edition has reached the mark before the group stage has even concluded. Eight of those 12 own goals have come against Arab national teams. To make matters worse for Tunisia, Brian Brobbey doubled the Netherlands’ lead moments later, leaving their World Cup campaign on the brink of a miserable conclusion. If these results hold, Netherlands will face Morocco in the last 32. Japan will meet Brazil and Sweden will go against France. A fairly straightforward opening half. Two early goals for the Netherlands, who pretty much had their way of things with possession from there. Japan and Sweden are drawing 0-0 in Arlington, meaning the Dutch will go through as Group F winners if the present results hold. 45 min The pace has slowed significantly here. The fourth official signals for three minutes of stoppage time. 42 min Reijnders eludes a couple of defenders inside the area before finding Gakpo, whose attempt to center is busted up by Dahmen. 38 min More good defending from Koeman’s side, who disrupt a Ben Slimane attempt at the crucial moment after Abdi found him for a tap-in. 35 min Gharbi carries down the right flank before sending in a cross for Mastouri, but Verbruggen snuffs it out easily (again). 32 min Another corner for the Netherlands, who are really laying it on thick. A couple of half-chances for the Dutch before Tunisia can finally clear. 29 min The corner is played outside the box for Malen, whose shot from about 22 yards curls well over and wide of the target. 28 min Gravenberch slips past a defender and has a go on goal, but it deflects off a Tunisia player for a corner. 26 min We’re back under way in Kansas City, which seems to have entirely dodged what could have been a rather dire weather situation. 22 min A lengthy sequence of unin
chineseTitle: 突尼斯 1-3 荷兰：2026 年世界杯——事实如此
chineseSummary: 详细报道：布赖恩·布罗比再次射门，荷兰队晋级 32 强，突尼斯队毫无意义地回家。布莱恩·阿门·格雷厄姆正在观看
chineseContent: 这就是今晚的全部内容。感谢您跟随我们，请务必查看埃德·阿伦斯在堪萨斯城的比赛报告。这是官方的。荷兰队在 F 组获胜后将在 32 强赛中对阵摩洛哥（瓜达卢佩，星期一，6 月 29 日）。日本队将迎战巴西队（休斯顿，6 月 29 日星期一）。瑞典将对阵法国、挪威、瑞士或德国。哨声一响，荷兰队3-1战胜突尼斯队，尽了自己的一份力量。他们会在阿灵顿得到他们想要的结果吗？看来距离他们只有几分钟的路程了……90+3分钟，突尼斯队赢得角球，但轻松脱险。第90分钟，突尼斯队进行最后一次换人，派出进球球员塞巴斯蒂安·图内克蒂（Sebastian Tounekti）替换哈泽姆·马斯图里（Hazem Mastouri）。将会有五分钟的补时时间。 86分钟 如果这样的结果再维持几分钟，荷兰队将积7分位居F组榜首，而日本队和瑞典队将双双出线。第 84 分钟，诺亚·朗 (Noa Lang) 换下科迪·加克波 (Cody Gakpo)。这是荷兰队的第五次也是最后一次换人。第80分钟，荷兰队获得25码处任意球。在阿凯的最后一脚射门偏出门柱之前，他的射门在禁区内进行了乒乓球运动。第77分钟，梅杰布里远射，维尔布鲁根全力伸展，皮球高出横梁。现在荷兰队将进行第四次换人，孟菲斯·德佩替换布莱恩·布罗比。第75分钟，突尼斯队第四次替补出场，比赛才刚刚恢复：菲拉斯·查瓦特（Firas Chaouat）替下伊斯梅尔·加尔比（Ismael Gharbi），后者几乎在第二分钟就首开纪录。第 72 分钟，荷兰队在断水区另一侧换了几名替补。萨默维尔、克鲁伊维特和库普迈纳斯入队。马伦、雷恩德斯和德容出局。 69 分钟 比赛暂停，再次补充水分。 68 分钟 雷金德斯最新射门后的时刻
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
